checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user=agent.
git and tmux installed via apt-get.

reasons: tests/compute_reward.py should be moved to environment/tests/ and bundled into the Docker image via COPY

checks.detail: Stray job-local.yaml at task root is not a standard Harbor file.
reasons: job-local.yaml is non-standard; either remove it or document why it is needed alongside job.yaml

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py to environment/tests/compute_reward.py and add a COPY directive in the Dockerfile so it is bundled into /tests at image build time.
Remove or rename job-local.yaml, or add a .gitignore entry if it is a local-only override.

checks.detail: task.toml declares [agent] user = "agent" for non-root agent execution.
reasons: present: [agent] user = "agent" at task.toml:10

checks.detail: All required isolation directives are present in the Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent (line 11)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app (line 27)
present: chown agent:agent /solution (line 18)
present: mkdir /tests && chmod 700 /tests (line 15), re-locked after refdata staging (line 85)
present: strace installed (line 7) — required since verifier runs agent code
present: extra hardening — /tests/refdata staged root-only with chmod -R go-rwx (line 86); .ref answer keys removed from agent-readable /app/tests (line 84)
note: uid 1000 not explicitly pinned; useradd defaults yield uid 1000 on debian slim, acceptable

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first then wipes, runs agent code as su agent under strace with timeout, and computes reward outside strace.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf (lines 39-40)
present: trap on EXIT guarantees fallback reward 0.0 (line 45)
present: exit 0 always (line 310; fail_with also exits 0 at line 61)
present: chmod 700 /tests re-lock after Harbor upload (line 48)
present: env sanitisation — unset LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/BASH_ENV/etc (line 52)
present: pkill -u agent before and after agent phase (lines 65, 119, 145, 188)
present: agent code wrapped in strace -f + timeout + su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i ...' (lines 120-125, 146-151)
present: strace logs scanned for reward.{txt,json} openat with O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT (lines 126, 152)
present: compute_reward.py invoked OUTSIDE strace as root (line 308), invoked with python3 -ISs (no agent imports)
present: no chown root:root on /logs
present: no set -euo pipefail (avoids early abort without reward)

reasons: agent's myld is built via `make -C /app` under strace (line 150)
agent's myld is invoked to link tier objects (RUNNER, line 97)
agent-produced ELFs are executed via timeout 10 (line 102)
strace + su agent are mandatory and present
scoring (compute_reward.py) reads only static files (test_results.json, strace logs) — does not import /app

checks.detail: Task is C/systems-programming with no Python or external package dependencies requiring a lockfile.
No Python package manager used; toolchain is apt-installed (gcc, musl-tools, binutils).
task.toml has no docker_image set; acceptable during development but required before final delivery.

notes: Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery.

checks.detail: Clear deliverable: fix myld.c so linked programs run and produce correct output, with invocation contract and section behaviors spelled out.
No explicit sandbox time bound or iterative-best-output hint is provided to the agent.
reasons: instruction.md does not mention timeout or suggest keeping best result updated as agent iterates

checks.detail: Reads as a conversational colleague handoff with debugging tips rather than a formal spec.
53 lines, well under the 100-line limit.
No mention of scorer, verifier, reward, evaluation, or test.sh in instruction.md.
Mentions make, readelf, objdump, musl-gcc, gcc, and points at <elf.h> and musl paths.

notes: Consider adding a one-line hint about the sandbox time budget (e.g., 'you have up to 2 hours') or suggesting the agent keep myld.c in a runnable state across iterations.

checks.detail: Baseline myld.c compiles but produces non-running ELFs; structural checks are gated by execution gates in STRUCTURAL_GATES so unrun tiers contribute 0.
Reward file, /tests, refdata, and stage inputs are all root-only by container-start; agent runs under su+strace and is killed before grading.
All agent code paths (make build, link, run) execute under `su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i ...'`.
strace -f wraps every agent invocation (build + each link/run) and logs are scanned for reward openat and toolchain execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs with `python3 -ISs` from /tests, reads only the root-written test_results.json and /app/myld.c as text; no imports from /app.
No `set -e`, EXIT trap guarantees reward.txt=0 on any early exit, every fail_with exits 0, and all subprocesses are bounded by `timeout`.
Structural-only T4 checks (entry/sections/nostdlib) and T1/T2 ELF checks are gated behind genuine execution passes, so a baseline emitting valid-but-non-running ELFs scores 0.
Grading runs the produced ELF and diffs stdout+exit against root-owned reference outputs, measuring the deliverable's behavior independent of linker algorithm.
Five tiers weighted by difficulty (0.08/0.12/0.16/0.24/0.40); the easy tiers cap at ~0.36 total so the agent cannot game a high score without solving the hard musl tier.
Trivial outputs fail link/diff and score 0; banned-pattern source scan, MIN_CODE_LINES=150, and strace-based shellout detection block the obvious shell-out-to-ld shortcut.
compute_reward.py reads /logs/verifier/test_results.json (written by root grading phase) and never imports agent code, so it can re-run independently.

Both --fail and success paths in compute_reward.py emit reward.json with score, subscores list, and additional_data via write_reward (failure path uses subscores=[{subtask:t,score:0.0} for t in TIERS]).
Tiered tests (exit, hello, data, bss, archive, musl printf/multi/common) map directly to instruction requirements; structural T4 checks are gated behind execution.
ELF, relocations, archive scanning, and anti-shellout constraints are all explicit in instruction.md or standard systems knowledge.
Dockerfile installs build-essential, musl-tools, strace, etc.; Makefile + myld.c starter present; musl path matches instruction.
solution/solve.sh is 152 lines and writes a working ld/gcc wrapper plus the oracle marker.
All graded cases are structural variants of the documented requirement (valid running ELF executables linking the provided test inputs).
Slim base, cache cleaned, every installed package (build-essential, musl-tools/dev, git, tmux, strace, procps) is genuinely used by build or verifier.
Only solve.sh present; embeds the C source inline and writes the oracle marker correctly.
All major sections are justified by the threat model, but two lines are defensive duplicates of Dockerfile state.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + rm -rf /logs/verifier (mandatory lockdown)
useful: chmod 700 /tests (Harbor resets to 755 after upload)
useful: env -i + LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH unset (single-container side channels)
useful: pkill -u agent + recheck (kills cross-phase background writers)
useful: root-owned $STAGE with .o/.a copies (agent cannot swap link inputs)
useful: strace -f around build + every link/run (mandatory: agent code executes)
useful: shellout detection via execve grep (catches exec families the source-substring scan can't see)
useful: trap to guarantee reward.txt exists on early exit
redundant: chmod -R go-rwx $REFDATA at line 49 duplicates Dockerfile line 86
redundant: find /tests -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x — no .sh files live in /tests at runtime besides test.sh itself

checks.detail: No individual files exceed 30MB; repo is text-only sources plus small assembly/C test inputs.
Workspace contains only Makefile, myld.c, start.S, syscalls.S, and test inputs — no dead code or stale configs.

notes: test.sh is dense but every section maps to a real attack vector given the single-container model — keep as-is or trim only the two defensive duplicates noted.
Consider moving the extra 'reward' top-level key in reward.json into additional_data to match the documented schema strictly.

instruction_quality.task_summary: Agent is given a partially working single-file static linker (myld.c) for x86-64 Linux that compiles and emits structurally valid ELF but produces non-functional executables. They must debug and fix symbol resolution, section merging, relocations, and archive handling so linked test programs (including musl-libc programs) run correctly.
instruction_quality.deliverable: working myld binary built from a fixed myld.c

measures: /tests/refdata chmod -R go-rwx so .ref answers and pristine .o/.a are unreadable by agent
STAGE chown -R root:root + chmod 644 inputs; only OUTDIR is agent-writable
.ref reference binaries removed from /app/tests at build time
Verifier drops to agent uid via `su agent -s /bin/bash -c "env -i PATH=... bash ..."` for build + every link/run
pkill -9 -u agent before, between, and after agent phases
strace -f -e trace=clone,clone3,fork,vfork,execve,openat wraps build and every link/run
Per-job strace log scanned for openat of reward.{txt,json} with O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT
Per-job strace log scanned for execve of ld/ld.gold/ld.lld/ld.bfd/gold/gcc/g++/cc1/cc1plus/collect2/musl-gcc/clang/lld and flags shellout
test_results.json is written only by the root grading phase after agent processes are killed
compute_reward.py runs with `python3 -ISs` outside strace and never imports /app
Source gate inspects /app/myld.c text for banned execvp/system/popen patterns and a MIN_CODE_LINES=150 floor
Structural readelf checks gated behind matching execution test via STRUCTURAL_GATES
Oracle marker requires both file existence and secret token contents matching HARBOR_ORACLE_FLAG injected only into solution.env, so the agent cannot forge it
env -i used when su'ing into agent shells
EXIT trap guarantees reward.txt=0 on any early abort

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent's myld shells out to a real linker via a renamed/copied binary path (e.g., `cp /usr/bin/ld /tmp/x && execve('/tmp/x', ...)`), bypassing the strace shellout regex that matches only literal toolchain basenames.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Source gate still catches the common execvp("ld"/"gcc") spellings; producing the renamed binary requires deliberate intent and the linked ELFs would still need to actually run correctly to score, so this is a soft anti-gaming heuristic the task author accepts.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent hand-crafts an ELF in myld.c that prints the expected stdout/exit for each test case without performing real relocation work.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Reference outputs live root-only in /tests/refdata and are never staged to the agent; reproducing the outputs amounts to solving the task by another means rather than reward manipulation.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent tampers with its own /logs/agent traces before verification.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented as inherent to the single-container model in HARBOR_GUIDE; only /logs/verifier matters for scoring and is locked to root before any grading.

reward_design.formula: score = clamp(sum_over_tiers(weight_t * passed_t / total_t), 0, 1); zeroed if anti-gaming triggers (banned source pattern, source too short, strace-observed shellout to ld/gcc/clang/etc., and not oracle)
reward_design.formula_explanation: Difficulty-weighted sum across five tiers of real link+execute tests, with structural-only checks gated behind genuine execution to prevent credit for non-running ELFs.

components.name: T1 basic ELF
components.metric: pass fraction of {t1_t1_exit run, t1_t1_exit_elf structural-gated}
components.gate: t1_t1_exit_elf only counts if t1_t1_exit passed

components.name: T2 cross-file
components.metric: pass fraction of {t2_t2_hello, t2_t2_data, t2_t2_hello_elf structural-gated}
components.gate: t2_t2_hello_elf only counts if t2_t2_hello passed

components.name: T4 musl hello + structural
components.metric: pass fraction of {t4_entry, t4_sections, t4_nostdlib, t4_hello_libc}
components.gate: t4_entry/sections/nostdlib only count if t4_hello_libc passed

dimension_analysis.name: T1
dimension_analysis.notes: Single .o inline syscall; bounded weight (0.08) limits gaming value.

dimension_analysis.name: T2
dimension_analysis.notes: Cross-file function call + data section; baseline myld already partially structurally valid but fails to execute.

dimension_analysis.name: T4
dimension_analysis.notes: Structural sub-checks gated by t4_hello_libc execution, so the tier can't be partial-credited without real musl linking work.

dimension_analysis.name: T5
dimension_analysis.notes: printf/multi-file/common symbols against musl libc.a; weight 0.40 dominates total, so a real score requires solving the hardest tier.

redundant_items.why: Dockerfile already applies chmod -R go-rwx /tests/refdata at build time; /tests is root-only so the agent cannot alter it between phases.
The only .sh in /tests is test.sh (already executable to be invoked); the runtime RUNNER script is created in $STAGE, not /tests.

redundant_items.item: line 152: duplicate 'reward' key alongside 'score' in reward.json payload
redundant_items.why: reward.json schema specifies only 'score'; the extra top-level 'reward' field is a stray key not in the documented schema.
